items.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran says it has closed the strait of Hormuz again due to US blockade
items.standfirst: In a statement carried by Iranian media, the Iranian military’s operational command said the strait had ‘reverted to its previous state’Separate to the Pakistani army chief’s trip to Iran (see post at 07:53), the Pakistani prime minister, Shehbaz Sharif, and foreign minister Ishaq Dar also concluded a trip to the Middle East after visiting Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkey for talks.“We have just concluded the last leg of our engagements following productive and fruitful visits … where we held meaningful bilateral discussions aimed at strengthening cooperation across key areas,” Dar said on X. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran reimposes Strait of Hormuz restrictions, citing U.S. blockade
items.standfirst: Iran swiftly reversed course on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. said the move would not end its blockade.
items.content: Iran swiftly reversed course on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. said the move would not end its blockade.

items.title: As oil prices plunge below $91 after weeks, a new Hormuz crisis emerges
items.standfirst: Brent crude falls over 9 percent after Iran said it will reopen waterway, only to shut it again over US blockade.
items.content: Brent crude falls over 9 percent after Iran said it will reopen waterway, only to shut it again over US blockade.

items.title: Iran closes Hormuz Strait again over US blockade with ships mid-transit
items.standfirst: Iran's military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again on Saturday, its military command said, hours after reopening it and with more than a dozen commercial ships passing through the vital waterway. The toing and froing over the strait cast doubt on US President Donald Trump's optimism the day before, that a peace deal to end the US-Israeli war with Iran was "very close". FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah has the latest from Tehran.
items.content: Iran's military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again on Saturday, its military command said, hours after reopening it and with more than a dozen commercial ships passing through the vital waterway. The toing and froing over the strait cast doubt on US President Donald Trump's optimism the day before, that a peace deal to end the US-Israeli war with Iran was "very close". FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah has the latest from Tehran.

items.title: Uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz remains, as ceasefire nears its end
items.standfirst: Iran's military said on Saturday the Strait of Hormuz has "returned to its previous state." The announcement came after President Trump had said the blockade on Iranian ports would remain in place.
items.content: Iran's military said on Saturday the Strait of Hormuz has "returned to its previous state." The announcement came after President Trump had said the blockade on Iranian ports would remain in place.(Image credit: Win McNamee)

items.title: Iran reimposes 'strict control' over Strait of Hormuz, citing continued U.S. naval blockade
items.standfirst: Iranian military officials said Saturday that conditions in the Strait of Hormuz have reverted to "strict control," citing the continued U.S. blockade of its ports, just one day after declaring the waterway “completely open.”“Control over the Strait of Hormuz has returned to its previous state, and this strategic waterway is now under strict management and control of the armed forces,” a spokesperson for Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters said.“Until America allows
items.content: Iranian military officials said Saturday that conditions in the Strait of Hormuz have reverted to "strict control," citing the continued U.S. blockade of its ports, just one day after declaring the waterway “completely open.”“Control over the Strait of Hormuz has returned to its previous state, and this strategic waterway is now under strict management and control of the armed forces,” a spokesperson for Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters said.“Until America allows

items.title: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' amid fragile ceasefire
items.standfirst: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open to commercial shipping during the Lebanon ceasefire, triggering a sharp drop in oil prices, while Donald Trump welcomed the move but kept a US naval blockade in place. At the same time, Emmanuel Macron and the UK are leading plans for a neutral mission to secure the route, as shipping firms remain cautious and seek further clarity.
items.content: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open to commercial shipping during the Lebanon ceasefire, triggering a sharp drop in oil prices, while Donald Trump welcomed the move but kept a US naval blockade in place. At the same time, Emmanuel Macron and the UK are leading plans for a neutral mission to secure the route, as shipping firms remain cautious and seek further clarity.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 50 of the US-Iran conflict?
items.standfirst: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again until US lifts its ports blockade, hours after Trump said he was optimistic of deal.
items.content: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again until US lifts its ports blockade, hours after Trump said he was optimistic of deal.

items.title: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
items.standfirst: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.
items.content: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

items.title: Middle East war live: Iranian military says closed Strait of Hormuz again over US blockade
items.standfirst: Iran's central military command said Saturday that it would resume "strict management" of the Strait of Hormuz after accusing the US of breaking commitments by maintaining a naval blockade on Iranian shipping. Tehran warned that restrictions will remain in place until freedom of movement is restored. Follow our live blog for the latest updates.
items.content: Iran's central military command said Saturday that it would resume "strict management" of the Strait of Hormuz after accusing the US of breaking commitments by maintaining a naval blockade on Iranian shipping. Tehran warned that restrictions will remain in place until freedom of movement is restored. Follow our live blog for the latest updates.

items.title: Iran says strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’ but sounds warning on US blockade
items.standfirst: Iran says strait could close again if US blockade continues, but Trump says it will remain in place until ‘transaction’ with Tehran is completeMiddle East crisis live: latest news updatesIran’s foreign minister has said that the strait of Hormuz is now fully open to commercial vessels, reinforcing hopes for an eventual end to the war in the Middle East and sending oil prices tumbling despite analysts’ warnings that there will be no immediate widespread resumption of passage through the vital waterway.In a barrage of social media posts, Donald Trump claimed on Friday that Iran had agreed never to close the strategic waterway again, hailing “A GREAT AND BRILLIANT DAY FOR THE WORLD!” Continue reading...
items.content: Iran says strait could close again if US blockade continues, but Trump says it will remain in place until ‘transaction’ with Tehran is completeMiddle East crisis live: latest news updatesIran’s foreign minister has said that the strait of Hormuz is now fully open to commercial vessels, reinforcing hopes for an eventual end to the war in the Middle East and sending oil prices tumbling despite analysts’ warnings that there will be no immediate widespread resumption of passage through the vital waterway.In a barrage of social media posts, Donald Trump claimed on Friday that Iran had agreed never to close the strategic waterway again, hailing “A GREAT AND BRILLIANT DAY FOR THE WORLD!” Continue reading...

items.title: Oil prices plummet after Iran says Strait of Hormuz is "completely open"
items.standfirst: Prices dropped after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.
items.content: Prices dropped after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

items.title: Stocks soar and oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait of Hormuz is open
items.standfirst: With word of the Strait of Hormuz reopening, the stock market soared on Friday. Oil prices dropped about 10%, and as a result, gas prices ticked downward. Kelly O'Grady breaks it all down.
items.content: With word of the Strait of Hormuz reopening, the stock market soared on Friday. Oil prices dropped about 10%, and as a result, gas prices ticked downward. Kelly O'Grady breaks it all down.

items.title: Trump says Iran peace deal could be reached ‘soon’ after Tehran reopens Hormuz Strait
items.standfirst: Tehran's announcement on Friday that the critical Strait of Hormuz was reopen to commercial shipping for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was followed by a flurry of social media posts by US President Donald Trump suggesting that a peace deal could be reached "soon". But Iranian officials said the two sides still needed to overcome serious differences.
items.content: Tehran's announcement on Friday that the critical Strait of Hormuz was reopen to commercial shipping for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was followed by a flurry of social media posts by US President Donald Trump suggesting that a peace deal could be reached "soon". But Iranian officials said the two sides still needed to overcome serious differences.

items.title: Oil price drops below $90 a barrel after Iran says strait of Hormuz is open
items.standfirst: Gas price also falls and markets rise amid hopes Donald Trump will succeed in reaching deal with TehranOil and gas prices fell sharply on Friday after Iran said the strait of Hormuz was open to commercial shipping, potentially clearing the way for tankers holding millions of barrels of oil and gas to reach the global market.Iran’s foreign minister said vessels would be free to transit the strait of Hormuz for the duration of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, which was struck on Thursday. Continue reading...
items.content: Gas price also falls and markets rise amid hopes Donald Trump will succeed in reaching deal with TehranOil and gas prices fell sharply on Friday after Iran said the strait of Hormuz was open to commercial shipping, potentially clearing the way for tankers holding millions of barrels of oil and gas to reach the global market.Iran’s foreign minister said vessels would be free to transit the strait of Hormuz for the duration of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, which was struck on Thursday. Continue reading...

items.title: Oil plunges to lowest price in weeks after Iran says it will fully open Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.
items.content: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.

clusters.title: Manhunt underway after bank heist in Italy holds 25 people hostage

bullet_summary: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system
Reported by NBC World, Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: Manhunt underway after bank heist in Italy holds 25 people hostage
items.standfirst: A manhunt is underway after robbers pulled off a heist that held 25 people hostage at a bank in Italy. The robbers escaped through a tunnel connected to the sewers, according to local media.
items.content: A manhunt is underway after robbers pulled off a heist that held 25 people hostage at a bank in Italy. The robbers escaped through a tunnel connected to the sewers, according to local media.

clusters.title: Singer d4vd arrested on suspicion of murder in death of Celeste Rivas, LAPD says
clusters.neutral_headline: Singer D4vd arrested on suspicion of murdering teenage girl
bullet_summary: Singer and songwriter d4vd has been arrested on suspicion of murder for the death of a 14-year-old whose dismembered body was found last year in a Tesla belonging to the singer
He was arrested after the body of a 14-year-old girl was found in the trunk of his Tesla in September 2025
The remains of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in the singer's car last year

items.title: What we know about the arrest of singer d4vd in teen girl's death
items.standfirst: David Burke, a singer who performs under the name d4vd, is behind bars on suspicion of murder. He was arrested after the body of a 14-year-old girl was found in the trunk of his Tesla in September 2025. Matt Gutman reports.
